_comment: Scope: the bridge carries ONLY the 3 cabin signals between VM1 and
VM2; battery and range are NOT bridged (the EV Range Extender app
on VM1 owns them locally and nothing on VM2 consumes them).
- all 3 cabin signals are BIDIRECTIONAL so the EV Range Extender
app on VM1 can write to VM1's Kuksa and have those values
mirrored to VM2 where the HVAC / Seat ECUs react, AND the
dashboard-driven values from VM2 still reach VM1.
The bridge's source-label filter (see kuksa_bridge.py) breaks
the otherwise-symmetric ping-pong; the receiver-side dedup
VM1 listens on tcp/0.0.0.0:7448 for the Zenoh peer connection from
VM2 (kept off the legacy 7447 port so zenoh_client.py keeps working
side-by-side during the cutover). The same single Zenoh connection

signals._role: VM1 <-> VM2: cabin (bidirectional - EV-app writes flow VM1 -> VM2)
VM1 <-> VM2: cabin (bidirectional - EV-app writes flow VM1 -> VM2)
VM1 <-> VM2: cabin (bidirectional - EV-app writes flow VM1 -> VM2)
